attacking_style: Launches attacks on steep gradients (12%+) immediately after regaining contact or when peloton regroups, exploiting moments of transition rather than sustained efforts. Prefers late accelerations after tactical hiding in breakaways.
team_tactics: Limited team support evident across multiple years - UAE required BORA assistance for chase control in 2019, suggesting thin domestique depth. When serving as domestique (2017), forced multiple accelerations to bleed break energy for team leaders.
terrain_preference: Excels on 12%+ steep gradients where explosive acceleration matters. Vulnerable on technical descents where crashes have cost stage wins and exposed GC weakness. Comfortable using descents tactically to hide in breaks.
vulnerability: Technical descending is a documented weakness (2017 crash while in winning break). Teams with insufficient chase depth expose him when defending GC positions. Energy depletion when performing extensive domestique work.
key_patterns: Attacks on 12%+ gradients immediately after regrouping or regaining contact (2 observations, 2019 Vuelta/Giro)
Uses tactical hiding in breaks, appearing to lose contact then regaining on descents (2019 Giro)
Crashes/struggles on technical descents, documented GC vulnerability in these conditions (2017 Giro)
Sacrifices stage wins for GC time when in maglia rosa position, prioritizes classification (2019 Giro)
Team lacks chase depth requiring external assistance to defend leader positions (2019 Giro)
Targets secondary classifications when GC neutralized, unusually prioritizes intermediate sprints (2017 Giro)
form_trajectory: positive: 2019 Vuelta showed tactical maturity with ruthlessly timed accelerations on steep terrain, advancing beyond earlier domestique role
